title: cephalotaxus harringtonia subsp. koreana
intro: Cephalotaxus harringtonia subsp. koreana, also known as Korean plum yew, is a subspecies of evergreen coniferous plant that belongs to the Taxaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to inhibit the growth of cancer cells and its anti-inflammatory effects, which make it beneficial for treating various health conditions such as arthritis and skin diseases. The plant contains taxol, a compound with potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting cancer, and improving immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health issues and promote overall well-being.
